LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RATIFICATION OF SEXUAL ABUSE OF A MINOR IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. A SCHOOL DISTRICT MAY NOT BE HELD VICARIOUSLY LIABLE FOR THE SEXUAL MISCONDUCT OF ITS EMPLOYEE. KIMBERLY M. V. LOS ANGELES UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT (1989) 215 CAL. APP. 3D 545 IS LEGALLY AND FACTUALLY ON POINT WITH THIS CASE AND COMPELS THE CONCLUSION OF NO VICARIOUS LIABILITY. ASSUMING WITHOUT DECIDING THAT A SCHOOL DISTRICT CAN BE HELD LIABLE FOR ITS SUPERVISORY OR MANAGING EMPLOYEE RATIFICATION OF THE SEXUAL MISCONDUCT BY ANOTHER EMPLOYEE, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED AND, WITHOUT CONTRADICTING THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, IS UNABLE TO ALLEGE THAT MS. JORDAN-OLIVER APPROVED OF THE ALLEGED SEXUAL MISCONDUCT OF MR. TOMSOVIC. (CACI 3710 (RATIFICATION REQUIRES APPROVAL OF THE CONDUCT WHICH WAS ALLEGEDLY RATIFIED)). THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT SHOW THAT MS. JORDAN-OLIVER DISAPPROVED OF THE ALLEGED SEXUAL MISCONDUCT OF MR. TOMSOVIC. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 =302/HK
MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-28-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-10-2016 Continued To Jan-11-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT -
Defendant San Francisco Unified School District's demurrer to the first cause of action for ratification of sexual abuse of a minor is sustained without leave to amend. A school district may not be held vicariously liable for the sexual misconduct of its employee. Kimberly M. v. Los Angeles Unified School District (1989) 215 Cal. App. 3d 545 is legally and factually on point with this case and compels the conclusion of no vicarious liability. Assuming without deciding that a school district can be held liable for its supervisory or managing employee ratification of the sexual misconduct by another employee, plaintiff has not alleged and, without contradicting the allegations of the first amended complaint, is unable to allege that Ms. Jordan-Oliver approved of the alleged sexual misconduct of Mr. Tomsovic. (CACI 3710 (ratification requires approval of the conduct which was allegedly ratified)). The allegations of the first amended complaint show that Ms. Jordan-Oliver disapproved of the alleged sexual misconduct of Mr. Tomsovic. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/HK
